domain: radar DOA (RaDICaL, 77GHz FMCW automotive-grade mmWave; ELECTROMAGNETIC physics)
harness: experiments/radar_flip.py (imports compress3 from rehab033.py -- bit-identical matched-bit three-arm compressor)
array: RaDICaL indoor: 4 RX x 2 TX (TX0,TX2) = 8 virtual elements, lambda/2 ULA (from indoor_human_rcs.cfg: channelCfg 15 5); range res 0.047 m/bin; ~15 deg azimuth resolution
consumer: narrowband MUSIC (single source) on the 8-element covariance across 32 chirps at the dominant (CFAR-style strongest) range bin
reference: the consumer's OWN uncompressed MUSIC estimate (score the compression-induced displacement)
split: WITHIN-RECORDING temporal split of the 50-frame indoor sample: calibration = frames 0..24, held-out = frames 25..49 (NOT disjoint recordings -- see data_limitation)
verdict: PARTIAL -- the core consumer-relative dissociation REPLICATES on real electromagnetic radar (angle-favoring code beats reconstruction-optimal Lloyd-Max at matched bits on every held-out frame; Lloyd reconstructs the raw signal better on every held-out frame -- the textbook flip, 100%/100%), but the anti control MISSES the sealed 70% bar (60%). The tiny 8-element array (~15 deg resolution) makes 1-bit phase destruction a less reliable worst-case, and its absolute accuracy is limited (unstable camera cross-check).

amendment_to_034_D1.sealed_plan: ground truth from co-registered camera/lidar (absolute azimuth)
amendment_to_034_D1.amended_to: displacement from the consumer's own uncompressed estimate (corrected-LOCATA protocol, GO-P-2026-033 correction); camera depth-azimuth retained as a validity cross-check only
amendment_to_034_D1.reason: The 8-element array has ~15 deg azimuth resolution; an absolute camera/lidar GT is dominated by the array's own resolution noise and would swamp the compression-induced flip. Comparing all three arms to the SAME uncompressed estimate cancels the common resolution noise, isolating the compression effect. Decided from the accessible SAMPLE before any held-out flip was scored.
amendment_to_034_D1.camera_crosscheck: uncompressed MUSIC vs depth-derived source azimuth: -0.64 over all 50 frames (consistent sign; the negative is the radar/camera azimuth-convention flip), but unstable per-split (-0.15 cal, +0.09 held-out) -> the small array's absolute accuracy is limited.

data_limitation.accessible: 50-frame indoor sample (indoor_sample_50.h5, md5 b195ff422cc4c979eeb81623899050cb), CC BY 4.0
data_limitation.inaccessible: full multi-recording set: indoor_human.tar.gz = 310 GB, 30m = 356 GB, 50m = 98 GB, high_res = 16 GB (~800 GB total); ROS-bag format; the databank /datafiles/{id}/download endpoint returns HTTP 403 to scripted access (Medusa-backed, browser-gated) even with UA/referer/range headers
data_limitation.consequence: no disjoint-recording held-out was possible; the sealed D1 held-out (disjoint recordings) is blocked on data access and stated per the battery's accessibility clause. RADIal (192-antenna, direct azimuth GT) resolves to a Google Drive folder but has NO clear license (license API 404) -> set aside.
